trends.name: Iran-US Military Escalation & Oil Markets
trends.summary: Real-time escalation: Trump administration striking Iran's Kharg Island oil hub while Iran's Foreign Minister signals readiness to defend and openness to Hormuz passage negotiations. IEA deploying emergency reserves to stabilize global oil markets. This directly impacts energy stocks, inflation expectations, and geopolitical risk premium across equities.

trends.name: Fed Rate Hike Possibility & Policy Pivot
trends.summary: Market sentiment is rapidly shifting from rate-cut expectations to potential Fed hikes as inflation pressures resurface. Wednesday's Fed decision, alongside PPI inflation data, will be critical; Vanguard's 4.8% long-term return forecast signals compressed valuations. This reversal reshapes everything from bond yields to growth vs. value positioning.

trends.name: Market Panic & Technical Deterioration
trends.summary: Financial sector showing 'spooky' technical signals; analysts warn panic is slowly gripping markets with selling expected to accelerate this week. S&P 500 approaching potential end of rally. Combined with geopolitical tensions, this signals heightened volatility and potential correction risk.

trends.name: Oil Surge & Retail vs. Institutional Divergence
trends.summary: Individual investors chasing oil's surge on Iran conflict headlines while institutions are positioning for longer-term outcomes and potential pullbacks. This retail-institutional divergence suggests crowded positioning risk and potential sharp reversals as narratives evolve.

trends.name: Nvidia GTC Event & AI Sector Valuation Test
trends.summary: Nvidia facing an exceptionally high bar at this week's GTC event given recent valuation compression and investor expectations. This is a critical test for the AI/semiconductor narrative and could trigger sector-wide repricing depending on guidance and announcements.

trends.name: Transportation Costs & FedEx Earnings Indicator
trends.summary: FedEx's transportation costs—driven heavily by oil prices—are a key bellwether for broader economic health and inflation pass-through. This week's earnings will signal whether oil spikes are manageable or margin-crushing across logistics and consumer goods.

trends.name: Banking Sector Weakness & Private Credit Risk
trends.summary: Bank stocks crushed this year with rising credit risk in private credit markets signaling potential contagion. Analysts identify 2 names expected to weather the storm, but broader sector stress reflects higher rates, deposit pressures, and credit deterioration concerns.

trends.name: Emerging Markets & Global Paradigm Shift
trends.summary: Investors reshaping EM allocations amid shifting global geopolitical and economic paradigm. China's industrial innovation focus and EM debt opportunity funds gaining attention as capital flows readjust in response to US-Iran tensions and policy uncertainty.

trends.name: Farm Labor & Immigration Policy Impact
trends.summary: Trump administration paradoxically tightening immigration while simultaneously loosening H2A visa access for farm labor to address critical agricultural shortages. This signals both labor market pressure and policy pragmatism, with implications for food prices, agribusiness costs, and inflation.

trends.name: High-Yield & Dividend Distributions Under Pressure
trends.summary: Multiple high-yield CEFs showcasing strong dividend sustainability (17% yields, 6-8% money machines) but risks rising as rate environment shifts. Distribution cuts possible if credit conditions deteriorate; investors must validate yield vs. capital preservation trade-offs.

trends.name: Housing & Leading Economic Indicators
trends.summary: February Pending Home Sales data due Tuesday as a forward-looking indicator of housing health. Combined with Philly Fed Manufacturing Index, these will provide crucial clues about economic momentum heading into potential rate hike scenario.

trends.name: Regulatory Risk & FCC/Free Speech Debate
trends.summary: FCC Chair Carr's broadcast license threat proposals triggering fierce Democratic opposition and free-speech debates. Regulatory uncertainty around media/tech could drive volatility in media, telecom, and tech stocks if policies shift under new administration.
